2605.06211.title: Contrastive Identification and Generation in the Limit
2605.06211.paper_summary: In the classical identification in the limit model of Gold [1967], a stream of positive examples is presented round by round, and the learner must eventually recover the target hypothesis. Recently, Kleinberg and Mullainathan [2024] introduced generation in the limit, where the learner instead must eventually output novel elements of the target's support. Both lines of work focus on positive-only or fully labeled data. Yet many natural supervision signals are inherently relational rather than singleton, which encode relationships between examples rather than labels of individual ones. We initiate the study of contrastive identification and generation in the limit, where the learner observes a contrastive presentation of data: a stream of unordered pairs $\{x,y\}$ satisfying $h(x)\ne h(y)$ for an unknown target binary hypothesis $h$, but which element is positive is hidden from the learner. We first present three results in the noiseless setting: an exact characterization of contrastive identifiable classes (a one-line geometric refinement of Angluin [1980]'s tell-tale condition), a combinatorial dimension called contrastive closure dimension (a contrasitive analogue of the closure dimension in Raman et al. [2025]) and exactly characterizing uniform contrastive generation with tight sample complexity, and a strict hierarchy in which contrastive generation and text identification are mutually incomparable. We then prove a sharp reversal under finite adversarial corruption: there exist classes identifiable from contrastive pairs under any finite corruption budget by a single budget-independent algorithm, yet not identifiable from positive examples under even one corrupted observation. The unifying technical object is the common crossing graph, which encodes pairwise ambiguity, family-level generation obstructions, and corruption defects in a single coverage-and-incidence language.
2605.06211.paper_summary_zh: 在Gold [1967]的極限模型中的經典識別中，正例流是逐輪呈現的，學習者最終必須恢復目標假設。最近，Kleinberg和Mullainathan [2024]引入了極限生成，其中學習者必須最終輸出目標支持的新元素。這兩條研究線路專注於僅正例或完全標記的數據。然而，許多自然監督信號本質上是關聯性的，而非單一的，這編碼了示例之間的關係，而不是單個示例的標籤。我們開始研究對比識別和極限生成，其中學習者觀察到數據的對比呈現：一串無序對$\{x,y\}$，滿足未知目標二元假設$h$的$h(x)\ne h(y)$，但哪個元素是正例對學習者來說是隱藏的。我們首先在無噪聲環境中提出三個結果：對比可識別類的精確特徵（Angluin [1980]的告密條件的一行幾何細化）、一個稱為對比閉合維度的組合維度（Raman等人[2025]中閉合維度的對比類比），以及精確描述具有緊湊樣本複雜度的均勻對比生成，並且在對比生成和文本識別之間存在嚴格的層級關係，彼此不可比擬。然後，我們在有限對抗腐敗下證明了一個尖銳的逆轉：存在類別可以在任何有限腐敗預算下通過單一不依賴預算的算法從對比對中識別出來，但在即使一個被腐敗的觀察下也無法從正例中識別出來。統一的技術對象是共同交叉圖，它在單一的覆蓋和發生語言中編碼了成對模糊性、家庭級生成障礙和腐敗缺陷。
2605.06211.author: Xiaoyu Li et.al.
2605.06211.authors: Xiaoyu Li, Andi Han, Jiaojiao Jiang, Junbin Gao
